title: Cladanthus scariosus
taxonomy: Cladanthus scariosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Cladanthus, a group of plants that are often considered to be part of the daisy family. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is also found in other parts of Europe and Asia. Cladanthus scariosus is a small, annual herb with narrow leaves and small yellow or white flowers. It is often found growing in dry, sandy soils and can tolerate a range of environmental conditions.
uses: Cladanthus scariosus, also known as sticky fleabane, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.
